title: Process Documentation & Training
excerpt: Hi - Has anyone set up a workspace for process documentation and training? We currently use Sweet Process but I was wondering if we could utilize Monday for these purposes as well. Individual tasks within a board, but I …

posts.author: Cwitt2 (Corey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi - Has anyone set up a workspace for process documentation and training? We currently use Sweet Process but I was wondering if we could utilize Monday for these purposes as well. Individual tasks within a board, but I don’t think that’s visually appealing enough for all users.

Thanks!

Corey

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m working on this now. I’m using a dashboard ‘board’ and then pulling in data from boards that have the links/processes. Using widgets such as ‘bookmarks’ and ‘tables’. Might play with that option?

posts.author: shirozayas (Shiro Zayas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Cwitt2,

For process documentation, I use WorkCanvas.com. This app is actually made by monday.com, and it’s fantastic for visually mapping out your processes. Think of it as a place where you can draw out how everything works, and then, here’s the best part, directly link those steps to action items on your monday.com boards.

Now, process documentation is a big umbrella. It covers everything from figuring out what your process even is, mapping it out, writing down those helpful Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and then getting everyone trained. So, depending on what you need most, I’d highly recommend diving into Monday Magic. You can actually build out a whole workspace that brings all these pieces together. It’s a really neat way to keep everything organized and make sure no part of your process or training gets left behind.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Clover_Gal - I’d love to see this in action. It’s something I’m currently working on, but each project varies, making it difficult to see how a plan can help with automated tasks across individual teams per responsible account, vs task board from date columns on various boards.

Almost depends on the project/process itself.
